MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-05-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ulla Pajala of BURNHAM BROWN (510-835-6731), appearing for defendant and moving party F.W. Spencer & Sons, Inc.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Theodora Douglas.

RULING: DEFENDANT F.W. SPENCER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant F.W. Spencer's Motion for Summary Judgment: Defendant's request for judicial notice (Exhibits A and B) is granted under Evidence Code section 452(d). Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Robert Douglas was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) It cannot be inferred that Plaintiff Steven Douglas would have been unable to recognize F.W. Spencer where no follow up questions were asked. (see Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2007) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1439; Ganoe v. Metalclad Insulation Corp. (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 1577, 1585.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439) (503/CML)
